recent_calls.summary: The user, Beverly Wise, called to schedule a regular service appointment for her AC unit, which is not cooling the house and may have a compressor issue. The agent collected all necessary details, scheduled a tentative appointment for Monday after 1 PM, and noted Beverly's interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs. The call ended positively with Beverly providing feedback and no further requests.

The user, Kim, called to inquire about gas line pressure tests for a commercial building experiencing a gas smell. The agent emphasized the potential emergency and advised contacting the gas company and 911 before proceeding. Kim agreed to continue, but the agent repeatedly stressed the importance of ensuring safety first.

The user, Alexander Reese, requested a callback from a technician to service his AC, which is not cooling properly and likely needs to be recharged. The agent confirmed the service details, contact information, and preferred callback time after noon, noting that the callback will occur on the next business day since it is after hours. The user declined to provide any additional information.
The user called after missing a call from a technician and requested to speak to a person. The agent confirmed the user's ZIP code and asked how to assist further before the user hung up.

Margaret inquired about the cost and process of a Freon refill for her system. The agent explained that pricing varies based on the amount and type of refrigerant needed and that technicians usually carry refrigerant to complete the refill in one visit. Margaret decided to consult her neighbor first and was informed about the best times to schedule an appointment if she chooses to proceed.
The call was very brief and disjointed, with the user and agent unable to complete a coherent conversation. The user hung up shortly after the call started without any clear resolution or request.

The user, Mark Campbell, reported an urgent cooling issue that needs immediate attention as it is getting hot inside. The agent documented the problem, prioritized the request for the next business day, and explained that no live teams are available after hours or on weekends. The user expressed frustration about repeated service failures and requested expedited service.

The user, Alberto Musalem, scheduled HVAC maintenance for two Honeywell high efficiency electronic air cleaners at his home in Missouri, preferring Thursday afternoon or Friday morning next week. He provided detailed information about the filters, his contact details, and expressed interest in home maintenance programs. The call ended positively with Alberto giving excellent feedback about the service.
The call was very brief and disconnected shortly after the agent introduced herself and asked for the ZIP code. The user did not provide any information or engage further.

The user called to cancel an appointment scheduled for Monday. The agent confirmed the cancellation request and informed the user that a team member would follow up to confirm. The call ended after the user expressed thanks and no further assistance was needed.

Mary Wolf called to report that her outside air conditioning unit is not working and requested a service appointment. The agent booked a provisional appointment for June 8th at 12 PM and confirmed coordination with American Home Shield. Mary provided positive feedback about the interaction and the booking was successfully completed.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

The user called to schedule a service for their AC and provided their ZIP code. The call ended abruptly after the user attempted to continue the conversation but was not responded to further by the agent.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Ron Evans called to request an emergency repair for his air conditioner, which is completely off and not cooling. The agent provisionally booked an appointment for Monday at 8 AM, the earliest available slot, and informed Ron that the team would confirm the exact time. Ron expressed urgency but accepted the scheduled time, and the call ended with no further questions.

The user, Norma Ogunmalawa, called to inquire about the technician's return to fix her air conditioner. The agent was unable to find her appointment in the system and offered to transfer her to a live representative, which the user accepted. The call was then successfully transferred to a live agent.

Monica Henson called to report water leaking from the bottom of her furnace when the AC is on, causing floors to soak. The agent informed her of the $99 diagnostic fee and scheduled an urgent service appointment for Monday morning. An attempt to transfer her to a live emergency specialist failed, but her issue was escalated and she was assured a callback.
The user called and requested to speak to a live agent. The agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and transferred the call to another live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a live operator, and the agent promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.

Vincent called to report an emergency with his air conditioning not working and causing damage to his floors. The agent confirmed the details and attempted to transfer the call to a live agent for immediate assistance. The call was successfully transferred for urgent help.

The user called to request AC service for a rental property at 858 Sugar Valley Court, Saint Peters, Missouri, reporting that the AC is not blowing cold. The agent gathered the necessary details and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent. The call was successfully transferred without further interaction.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, facilitated the transfer without any issues.

Ronald called to report that his brand new furnace, purchased from Budget Heating, has stopped working and needs immediate service due to his bedridden wife. The agent acknowledged the urgency and transferred the call to customer service for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.
The call was very brief and ended immediately after the agent's initial greeting and request for the ZIP code. The user hung up without responding or engaging further.

Vincent called to request a technician to switch his air conditioning system from heat to cool, as instructed by a previous technician. The agent noted his preference for early morning the next day and informed him that the team would confirm availability during business hours. Vincent was satisfied and thanked the agent before ending the call.

Manuel called on behalf of Janice Stuller to request expedited processing of a recall work order for air conditioning repair due to a medical emergency. The agent logged the request and informed Manuel that the team would review and follow up on the next business day. The call ended with confirmation that the request would be prioritized.

The user called to inquire about pricing and details for a plumbing conversion from a tub to a shower, including questions about licensing, permits, and VA HISA grant eligibility. The agent collected the user's contact information and noted the service address as Berkeley, Missouri, and arranged for a team member to follow up the next business day. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided feedback preferring to speak directly with a person.

The user, Predrag, called to report an issue with submitting an online form for air conditioning service. The agent documented the problem and informed the user that a representative will call back on the next business day since live transfers were unavailable after hours. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further assistance requested.

The user called to schedule a cooling service in Illinois. The agent confirmed the service type and informed the user that plumbing services are not offered in the area. The call ended abruptly after the user repeated their request several times without further response from the agent.

The user, Miranda Campschrader, called to report an issue with ice buildup on her air conditioner and requested service as soon as possible. The agent collected her contact details and service address, confirmed the service request, and informed her that the team would follow up. Miranda declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The call was very brief, with the user only saying "Two for" before hanging up. The agent introduced themselves but did not get a chance to assist the user further.
The user called to inquire about payment consent at the time of service. The agent was unable to access billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

The user called to reach the estimator who visited their house and requested to be connected to a live estimator. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate estimator. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.

Michael called to schedule an air conditioner repair through American Home Shield but did not have a dispatch number. The agent collected all necessary details and informed him that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him soon to confirm the appointment. Michael declined information about maintenance programs and expressed some frustration about previous response times before the call was transferred to a live agent.
The user called and requested to speak to Nicole. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to the appropriate person.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The user called and requested to speak to a human agent. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Dave from American Home Shield, called and requested to speak with a live agent. The agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user called to schedule a service order and provided their ZIP code. However, the call ended abruptly when the user expressed frustration and hung up. The agent was unable to complete the service request.

The user initially provided their ZIP code and expressed a desire to schedule an appointment, but the call was disrupted by repeated interruptions and the user expressing frustration with the AI agent. The call ended abruptly without resolution.

The user called to inquire about the status of a technician scheduled to fix their furnace, expressing concern about the delay. The agent attempted to assist but transferred the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user called to inquire about the timing of a repair appointment, expressing frustration about a delay after being told they were next. The agent acknowledged the frustration and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about service availability but provided ZIP codes that are not in the agent's service area. The user confirmed they only have one ZIP code, and the agent informed them that service is not available there. The call ended without further action.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.

Patrick Wadlow called to check if the technician was still scheduled for 2 PM. The agent could not find the appointment in the system and offered to transfer Patrick to a live representative, which he accepted. The call was then successfully transferred to another agent.
The user requested to speak with an operator, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
